purpose: PreToolUse hook on Write — the most comprehensive schema enforcer. Validates every structural invariant of dashboards/{id}/initialization.json before the write lands: valid JSON, default-template allowance, task.{name,type,created,total_tasks,total_waves} required & well-typed, name kebab-case, type ∈ {Waves,Chains}, agents[] non-empty (catches the 'wave headers with no cards' bug), waves[] non-empty, total_waves==waves.length, total_tasks==agents.length, sum(waves[].total)==agents.length, agent.id format ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+r?$, no duplicate IDs, every agent.wave matches a waves[].id, every depends_on entry references an existing agent.id, and (Chains mode) every agent appears in exactly one chain's tasks[]. Bypass: SYNAPSE_SKIP_SCHEMA=1 env var. Block reason aggregates all errors with newlines.
gotchas: Only validates Write — Edit uses partial patches that are harder to validate (caught by other hooks)
ide dashboard and Archive/ paths are skipped — those have non-swarm shapes
SYNAPSE_SKIP_SCHEMA=1 is the documented escape hatch — use sparingly for scratch/repair work
Block reason is JSON-escaped via `jq -R -s` so newlines render properly in the agent's view
Default-template detection (task=null, agents=[], waves=[]) lets ensureDashboard() write the empty template
agent.id format `^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+r?$` — repair tasks have trailing `r`
Block message points at agent/master/initialization_blueprint.md for the canonical schema
patterns: Aggregate-then-fail validation: collect all errors into ERRORS string, single block at end with all violations
jq cross-field assertions (sum(waves[].total) == agents.length, every wave referenced exists, etc.)
conventions: Error format: bullet point per violation prefixed with `\n- `
Block reason JSON-escaped to preserve formatting
